title: 11 Herbal Creams For Wheezing
intro_desc: If you experience wheezing, you know how uncomfortable it can be. Herbal creams can help. These creams often contain ingredients like eucalyptus, thyme, and rosemary. Eucalyptus globulus, in particular, is a common ingredient. It's known for its decongestant properties and its ability to ease breathing. When you inhale the vapors from eucalyptus, it helps to open up your airways, making it easier to breathe. This is why eucalyptus is often used in inhalers and steam inhalers to relieve wheezing.  Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, is another herb that can help with wheezing. It has antimicrobial properties, which means it can help fight off infections that may be causing your wheezing. Rosemary, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in your airways and make it easier to breathe. When you use a cream containing these herbs, the ingredients are absorbed into your skin and released into your lungs, helping to ease your wheezing.  Using herbal creams for wheezing can be beneficial for several reasons. For one, they can be a natural alternative to prescription medications, which can have side effects. They can also be more convenient to use than inhalers or other devices. Plus, they can be used in combination with other treatments, such as medication or therapy, to help manage your wheezing. Additionally, using herbal creams can be a comforting way to manage your symptoms, especially if you're feeling anxious or stressed about your wheezing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains the active constituents eucalyptol, camphor, and cineole. These compounds help open up the airways and relieve congestion by thinning mucus and reducing inflammation. Eucalyptol, in particular, has a decongestant property that helps ease wheezing by breaking down thick mucus and making it easier to expel. Cineole also has a bronchodilatory effect, which means it helps relax the muscles around the airways and allows air to pass through more freely. By combining these properties, eucalyptus globulus creams can provide quick relief from wheezing and other respiratory issues.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in the eucalyptus and peppermint essential oils.
Pour the mixture into a clean, airtight container. Let it cool and harden completely.
Once hardened, your Eucalyptus globulus cream is ready to use. Apply as needed to help relieve wheezing and congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the swelling in the airways that can cause wheezing. Thymol also has bronchodilatory effects, meaning it helps to widen the airways and make it easier to breathe. Carvacrol has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and widening the airways, thymus vulgaris creams can help to alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water and 1/4 cup of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a bowl, discarding the Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Add 1/4 cup of coconut oil or shea butter to the liquid and mix well.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool before applying it to the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation in the airways. This anti-inflammatory effect can help to alleviate wheezing symptoms by opening up air passages and making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the camphor and borneol present in Rosmarinus officinalis creams have a bronchodilatory effect, which means they can help to widen the airways and improve airflow. The anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties of these constituents work together to help relieve wheezing symptoms and promote easier breathing. By applying Rosmarinus officinalis creams topically, you can harness the anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory powers of these active constituents to help manage wheezing.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals until smooth.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Add the Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely before use. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways. The phenolic and terpenoid compounds present in ginger also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. The warming effect of ginger can help loosen mucus and ease breathing, making it easier to expel air from the lungs. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, Zingiber officinale creams may help alleviate wheezing and other respiratory symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add Zingiber officinale powder and stir until well combined.
Remove from heat and add shea butter and vitamin E oil. Stir until the mixture cools and thickens.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before applying to the skin to relieve wheezing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains bioactive constituents like rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and luteolin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, easing wheezing symptoms. Rosmarinic acid, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, such as histamine and leukotrienes, which contribute to wheezing. The antioxidant properties of luteolin and caffeic acid help to protect the lungs from oxidative stress, further alleviating wheezing symptoms. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Salvia officinalis creams may provide relief from wheezing and other respiratory issues.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the dried Salvia officinalis leaves to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, squeezing the leaves to extract as much liquid as possible.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, use as a cream to relieve wheezing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and chicoric acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the airways and ease wheezing. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams have a bronchodilatory effect, meaning they can help open up airways and improve breathing. Caffeic acid and chicoric acid have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation. By reducing inflammation and opening up airways, Echinacea purpurea creams can help alleviate wheezing and improve respiratory health.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a clean glass bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until it reaches 160°F to 180°F.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the heated coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the liquid mixture and heat it gently until the beeswax is melted, stirring constantly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains the bioactive constituents linalool and linalyl acetate. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which can help relax airway muscles and reduce wheezing. The linalool in Lavandula angustifolia creams also has antispasmodic properties, which can help calm down spasms in the airway muscles, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the calming effects of linalyl acetate can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for wheezing. By using Lavandula angustifolia creams, individuals may experience relief from wheezing symptoms due to its soothing and relaxing properties.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil (Lavandula angustifolia), and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add distilled water to the melted mixture and stir well. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly.
Add lavender essential oil and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, your Lavandula angustifolia cream is ready to use for wheezing relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhetinic acid and flavonoids as its active constituents. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Glycyrrhetinic acid has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the respiratory tract, while flavonoids have antioxidant properties that help to protect the lungs from oxidative stress. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help to alleviate wheezing caused by respiratory issues such as asthma. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these compounds work together to provide relief from wheezing and promote overall lung health.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until beeswax is fully melted.
Add glycerin and glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add vitamin E oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean container.
Let the mixture cool and solidify completely. Once solid, use a spoon to scoop out a small amount and apply it to the affected area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains active constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce wheezing by decreasing inflammation in the airways. Curcumin also has a bronchodilatory effect, which means it can help relax the muscles in the airways and improve breathing. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress in the lungs, which can contribute to wheezing. By reducing inflammation and improving airway function, Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa), 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until fully melted.
In a separate bowl, mix turmeric powder, shea butter, and olive oil until well combined.
Slowly add the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture to the turmeric mixture, stirring constantly until smooth.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains the bioactive constituents menthol and limonene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help relieve wheezing by reducing inflammation in the airways and making it easier to expel mucus. The menthol in mentha x piperita creams also has a bronchodilatory effect, which means it helps open up airways and improve breathing. By reducing inflammation and making it easier to breathe, mentha x piperita creams can help alleviate wheezing symptoms. The limonene in mentha x piperita creams also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the airways from damage caused by free radicals.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes. Stir in peppermint essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers or a silicone mold. Let it cool and solidify completely.
Once solid, label and store the creams in a cool, dry place. Apply a small amount to the chest or nose to help relieve wheezing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata creams contains boswellic acids, including acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (AKBA) and 11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (KBA). These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which can help to reduce wheezing. AKBA and KBA block the production of leukotrienes, chemicals that cause airway constriction and inflammation, thereby opening up airways and improving breathing. Additionally, boswellic acids have been shown to inhibit the activity of an enzyme called 5-lipoxygenase, which is involved in the production of leukotrienes. By reducing inflammation and airway constriction, Boswellia serrata creams may help to alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Boswellia serrata herbal creams for wheezing
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Boswellia serrata powder, 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the Boswellia serrata powder to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Add the peppermint essential oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden completely. Once set, your Boswellia serrata cream is ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease wheezing symptoms. Peppermint tea, for example, can help relax airways and reduce congestion. Ginger tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can also help soothe respiratory issues. By consuming these teas regularly, some people find their wheezing episodes become less frequent.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for wheezing can be a good option, but it's essential to be cautious. Some teas, like chamomile, may help calm your airways, while others, like peppermint, can irritate them. Drinking teas every day can lead to over-consumption of certain compounds, potentially causing more harm than good.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with wheezing symptoms, but it's hard to predict exactly when they'll start working. Some people notice a difference after just a few days, while others may take up to two weeks to feel the effects. It really depends on the individual and the type of herbal tea being used.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for wheezing can be most effective in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the tea's soothing properties to work throughout the day, helping to reduce wheezing and discomfort. As the body's natural rhythms tend to slow down in the evening, the tea's benefits may be less noticeable at night.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for wheezing. For example, if you drink tea with ginkgo biloba while taking medications that thin your blood, it may increase the risk of bleeding. This is because ginkgo biloba can also thin your blood. Be cautious when combining herbal teas with medications to avoid any potential problems.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant and wheezing, it's best to be careful with herbal teas. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally safe in small amounts, but others, like foxglove and licorice root, can be problematic. It's a good idea to check the label and choose teas from trusted brands, and to talk to your body about how you feel after trying a new tea.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for wheezing can be a helpful relief, but it's essential to choose ones that are safe during breastfeeding. Peppermint and ginger teas are often recommended, but be cautious with those containing eucalyptus, as it can be too strong. Look for gentle, organic options to ensure a soothing cup without passing on any risks to your baby.
title_2: COPD patients with wheezing: 11 natural herbal creams
